response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a capable polyglot engineer with strong craftsmanship values and a long track record across web, mobile, and embedded systems, but her technical profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role. Her primary stack is TypeScript and Node.js, with secondary experience in DLang and C/C++; there is no meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise demonstrated, no JVM-depth evident, and no exposure to Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework. The brief Java mentions (robotic arms at Altom, mobile orchestration tooling) do not indicate the deep JVM understanding required. Her only IDE-adjacent work was writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI — a C IDE, not a JVM-based one — which provides minimal transferable signal for this position.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) that would transfer well to a quality-focused engineering team
Prior work at National Instruments on IDE tooling (LabWindows/CVI) shows some familiarity with the domain of developer tools, even if in a different ecosystem
Active open-source contributor and community educator (workshops, talks, multiple maintained libraries) indicating strong communication and knowledge-sharing habits
concerns: No Java or Kotlin expertise evident; primary languages throughout career are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — the core technical requirement is entirely unmet
Zero exposure to the Spring ecosystem, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework mentioned in the job description
No experience with JVM internals, JVM-based IDE tooling, or language tooling for Java/Kotlin; the IDE experience at NI was C/C++-based and does not bridge this gap

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
IDE tooling exposure at NI (C/C++ domain)
